title: The 3rd Impacts
wikitext:
[]: The 3rd Impacts             Quote Post by Oblivious » Wed Jul 06, 2005 10:09 am Wooo joy. Another hypothesis. The movie ending and the series ending seem to be quite similar. Shinji realises that the world he created for himself was actually unneeded through his reflections the aforementioned world. I am not challenging that they were indeed vastly different, I am just drawing attention to what I believe Anno considered the most important part of Third Impact. Would I be correct in saying that the following observation is common to both Impacts? Through the things he sees and understands in the complemented world, Shinji grows stronger. His resulting psychological tempering renders the complemented world obsolete. Regarding the series ending: "I can learn to love myself." Once he can do this, what would be the point of the complemented world to the now empowered Shinji? The breaking glass and disappearing of the theater would then suggest the breaking of the complimented world, the same way Shinji rejects the one in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Wed Jul 06, 2005 10:36 am i was under the impression that the two different endings were practically the same for most purposes, but were simply made differently

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 06, 2005 11:31 am Third Impact s ? Plural?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Wed Jul 06, 2005 1:46 pm he is referring to the different endings (tv-series and movie)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 06, 2005 2:31 pm Just in case it's needed, the obvious citation here - MDWigs' An argument for the concurrent nature of episodes 26 and 26' My own doubts are best exprssed as the fact the EoE plays the latter bits in minor key, compared with EoTV.

Article: The 3rd Impacts
[]: The 3rd Impacts             Quote Post by Oblivious » Wed Jul 06, 2005 10:09 am Wooo joy. Another hypothesis. The movie ending and the series ending seem to be quite similar. Shinji realises that the world he created for himself was actually unneeded through his reflections the aforementioned world. I am not challenging that they were indeed vastly different, I am just drawing attention to what I believe Anno considered the most important part of Third Impact. Would I be correct in saying that the following observation is common to both Impacts? Through the things he sees and understands in the complemented world, Shinji grows stronger. His resulting psychological tempering renders the complemented world obsolete. Regarding the series ending: "I can learn to love myself." Once he can do this, what would be the point of the complemented world to the now empowered Shinji? The breaking glass and disappearing of the theater would then suggest the breaking of the complimented world, the same way Shinji rejects the one in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Wed Jul 06, 2005 10:36 am i was under the impression that the two different endings were practically the same for most purposes, but were simply made differently

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 06, 2005 11:31 am Third Impact s ? Plural?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Wed Jul 06, 2005 1:46 pm he is referring to the different endings (tv-series and movie)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 06, 2005 2:31 pm Just in case it's needed, the obvious citation here - MDWigs' An argument for the concurrent nature of episodes 26 and 26' My own doubts are best exprssed as the fact the EoE plays the latter bits in minor key, compared with EoTV.
